summary: One confirmed privacy blocker remains. Two warnings need product and target-level review before this build is ready for App Store Connect.

findings.title: Camera flow has no purpose string
findings.reason: The release flow requests access to a protected resource without a specific explanation of why the app needs it.
findings.fix: Add NSCameraUsageDescription in the Expo iOS configuration and explain that the camera scans parcel labels to add tracking numbers.
findings.verification: Build the release archive, inspect the merged Info.plist, then trigger the label scanner on a clean device.

findings.title: Widget privacy declaration needs target-level verification

evidence.excerpt: No PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy was found in the extension source folder.

findings.reason: Privacy manifests are target-sensitive. The app target manifest does not prove that the extension ships the required declaration.
findings.fix: Verify the extension target resources, then add the Required Reason API declaration at the widget source of truth if the archive does not contain it.
findings.verification: Export the archive and inspect the widget bundle for PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy and the declared UserDefaults reason.

findings.title: Primary empty states look unfinished
findings.reason: Two high-traffic screens use the same placeholder copy and provide no next action, which makes the product feel generic during first review.
findings.fix: Give each state a specific explanation and action, such as scanning the first parcel or adding a tracking number manually.
findings.verification: Launch with a new account and confirm each empty state explains the screen and leads to a working next step.

findings.title: Review notes can make the widget easier to find

evidence.excerpt: The app includes a home screen widget.

findings.reason: The note names the feature but does not tell a reviewer how to add it or what successful data looks like.
findings.fix: Add exact widget installation steps and the demo parcel that should appear.
findings.verification: Follow the notes from a clean simulator without using repository knowledge.

manual_checks.title: Subscription terms and restore path
manual_checks.reason: Static source cannot prove the storefront terms, localized price, and restored entitlement state shown by the release build.
manual_checks.verification: Use a sandbox account to purchase, cancel, reinstall, and restore on iPhone and iPad.

manual_checks.title: App Privacy answers match production data flows
manual_checks.reason: Repository signals do not establish every analytics payload, retention period, or linked-data choice in App Store Connect.
manual_checks.verification: Trace production analytics and crash payloads, then compare them with the submitted privacy answers.

manual_checks.title: Core tracking path works on iPad
manual_checks.reason: The repository does not prove layout, rotation, keyboard, or split-view behavior in the submitted build.
manual_checks.verification: Complete add, edit, notification, and delete flows on a supported iPad in portrait, landscape, and split view.

limitations: No App Store Connect metadata export was supplied.
Subscription and notification paths were not executed on a release build.
The sample contains fictional project evidence for demonstration only.

dimensions.name: Product distinction
dimensions.evidence: The carrier exception timeline and shared parcel workflow are product-specific, but the first-run state hides them.

dimensions.name: Binary and asset provenance
dimensions.evidence: No sibling or prior rejected project was supplied for exact asset comparison.

dimensions.name: Visual identity and accessibility
dimensions.evidence: A consistent tracking-status system is present. Dynamic Type and VoiceOver still need device verification.

dimensions.name: Microcopy and interaction states
dimensions.evidence: Two primary empty states repeat placeholder copy and offer no route into the core workflow.

dimensions.name: Product-page specificity
dimensions.evidence: No screenshots, subtitle, promotional text, or description were included in the review scope.

reviewer_experience.label: First launch reaches the tracking list
reviewer_experience.detail: Supported by the supplied screen recording.

reviewer_experience.label: Camera permission appears after an explanation
reviewer_experience.detail: Retest after adding the purpose string.

reviewer_experience.label: Demo parcel shows a carrier exception
reviewer_experience.detail: A stable demo tracking number is still needed.

app_review_notes:
Demo account: <REVIEW_EMAIL> / <REVIEW_PASSWORD>

To test label scanning, open Track, tap Add Parcel, then choose Scan Label. The camera is requested only after the explanation screen.

To test the widget, add ParcelTrack from the Home Screen widget gallery. The demo account contains one parcel in an exception state.

fix_groups.title: Submission blockers
items: Add and verify the camera purpose string in the merged release Info.plist.

fix_groups.title: Target and reviewer-path checks
items: Verify the widget privacy manifest in the archive.
Run subscription, privacy-answer, and iPad checks.
Complete the widget navigation in App Review Notes.

fix_groups.title: Product craft
items: Replace generic empty states with screen-specific explanations and actions.

disclaimer: Fictional sample based on public guidance. Approval is not guaranteed. Not affiliated with Apple.
